message:
test(indexer): generation manifest persist + serveable contract (ibuuh.30)

Adds rebuild_tantivy_from_db_persists_serveable_generation_manifest,
the sibling test to rebuild_tantivy_from_db_emits_equivalence_evidence,
pinning the ibuuh.30 publish-readiness contract end-to-end:

- Runs rebuild_tantivy_from_db on the seeded lexical fixture.
- Loads the persisted lexical-generation-manifest.json via
  lexical_generation::load_manifest and asserts:
    * is_serveable() — build_state == Validated and
      publish_state == Published.
    * indexed_doc_count matches both outcome.indexed_docs and the
      equivalence accumulator document_count (bridges ibuuh.29 →
      ibuuh.30 correctness).
    * equivalence_manifest_fingerprint points at the exact
      accumulator digest the rebuild emitted this run — validation
      tooling can diff generations without re-reading the ledger.
    * generation_id, attempt_id, source_db_fingerprint all non-empty.
    * failure_history is empty on a clean rebuild.
    * updated_at_ms >= created_at_ms monotonicity.
- Asserts the "lexical generation manifest published" INFO log and
  the presence of generation_id= + attempt_id= fields so operators
  can correlate manifest artifacts with rebuild runs.

Covers acceptance criterion 1 ("versioned scratch generations with
explicit manifests, validation state, publish state, and enough
metadata to audit source fingerprint, counts, build attempt
identity") end-to-end. Remaining ibuuh.30 work (scratch-directory
isolation, atomic rename-based promotion, kill-window crash tests)
is still open.

Validation: cargo test --lib
rebuild_tantivy_from_db_persists_serveable_generation_manifest passes;
cargo fmt --check clean on my file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
